audit_items.proof_trace: When `mdb_env_create(&env)` fails (e.g., ENOMEM via calloc failure), it returns without setting `*env`, leaving the stack-allocated `env` variable uninitialised. `mdb_env_close(env)` is then called unconditionally at line 79; since garbage != NULL it bypasses the NULL guard in mdb_env_close (mdb.c:5173) and immediately dereferences `env->me_dpages`, causing undefined behaviour/crash. Contrast with mdb_stat.c which correctly early-exits on mdb_env_create failure.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker or unprivileged user who can invoke `mdb_copy` under memory pressure (OOM) causes the process to crash via the uninitialised pointer dereference; however, external LDAP network reachability does not apply since mdb_copy is a standalone CLI utility, limiting this to a local crash/DoS scenario.
